what: Classify Points Relative to a Boundary Line — classify_boundary
title: Classify Points Relative to a Boundary Line — classify_boundary
text: Classifies data points boundary defined set coordinates. function uses piecewise linear interpolation determine boundary's y-value x-coordinate data, classifies points based whether lie boundary.

title: Classify Points Relative to a Boundary Line — classify_boundary
Classify Points Relative to a Boundary Line — classify_boundary
text: data data frame containing least two columns named 'x' 'y' representing coordinates points classified. boundary_coord two-column matrix data frame first column contains x-coordinates second column contains y-coordinates defining boundary line. Points ordered x-coordinate proper interpolation. classes character vector length 2 specifying class labels. Default c("", ""). first element assigned points boundary, second points .

title: Classify Points Relative to a Boundary Line — classify_boundary
text: character vector length number rows data, containing class labels point.

title: Classify Points Relative to a Boundary Line — classify_boundary
text: function uses stats::approxfun() rule = 2 piecewise linear interpolation. means: boundary points, linear interpolation used x-values outside range boundary coordinates, boundary extrapolated linearly using slope nearest endpoint Points exactly boundary (y == boundary_y) classified "" (second class).

title: Classify Points Relative to a Boundary Line — classify_boundary
code: # Create sample data data <- data.frame(   x = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),   y = c(2, 4, 3, 5, 1) )  # Define a simple linear boundary boundary <- data.frame(   x = c(1, 5),   y = c(2, 4) )  # Classify points classify_boundary(data, boundary) #> [1] "Below" "Above" "Below" "Above" "Below"  # Use custom class labels classify_boundary(data, boundary, classes = c("High", "Low")) #> [1] "Low"  "High" "Low"  "High" "Low"   # Complex boundary with multiple segments boundary_complex <- data.frame(   x = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),   y = c(1, 3, 2, 4, 3) ) classify_boundary(data, boundary_complex) #> [1] "Above" "Above" "Above" "Above" "Below"

what: Create a 2D Synthetic Dataset With Class Labels — create_data
title: Create a 2D Synthetic Dataset With Class Labels — create_data
text: Generates synthetic two-dimensional dataset using random uniform values. point assigned class label based whether lies coordinate thresholds provided. Coordinates can supplied directly matrix/data frame indirectly via JSON file containing array coordinate pairs.

title: Create a 2D Synthetic Dataset With Class Labels — create_data
code: create_data(   coord_filepath = NULL,   coord = NULL,   n_samples = 10000,   type = "uniform",   seed = 1835 )

title: Create a 2D Synthetic Dataset With Class Labels — create_data
text: coord_filepath character string specifying file path JSON file containing list/array coordinate pairs. Optional coord supplied. coord matrix data frame two columns (x y) specifying threshold coordinates. Optional coord_filepath supplied. n_samples Integer; number synthetic 2D points generate. type String; points generated. Can either 'uniform' 'grid' seed Integer; Random seed used generating dataset

title: Create a 2D Synthetic Dataset With Class Labels — create_data
text: data frame three columns: x Random uniform x-coordinate y Random uniform y-coordinate class Binary class label (0 1)

title: Create a 2D Synthetic Dataset With Class Labels — create_data
text: point labelled class = 1 coordinate thresholds : $$x > coord[,1] \; \text{} \; y > coord[,2]$$ Otherwise, point labelled 0.

title: Create a 2D Synthetic Dataset With Class Labels — create_data
Shiny bindings for sillysplines — sillysplinesOutput
text: Output render functions using sillysplines within Shiny applications interactive Rmd documents.

title: Shiny bindings for sillysplines — sillysplinesOutput
code: sillysplinesOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  renderSillysplines(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for sillysplines — sillysplinesOutput
text: outputId output variable read width, height Must valid CSS unit (like '100%', '400px', 'auto') number, coerced string 'px' appended. expr expression generates HTML widget (promise HTML widget). env environment evaluate expr. quoted expr quoted expression (quote())? useful want save expression variable. @return Returns htmlwidgets::shinyRenderWidget used server section shiny app connect sillysplinesOutput defined .

title: Shiny bindings for sillysplines — sillysplinesOutput
text: Returns htmlwidgets::shinyWidgetOutput used within UI shiny app.

title: Shiny bindings for sillysplines — sillysplinesOutput
code: library(shiny) app <- shinyApp(   ui = fluidPage(sillysplinesOutput('splines')),   server = function(input, output) {     splines_widget <- sillysplines()     output$splines= renderSillysplines(splines_widget)   } )  if (interactive()) app
